author: @YoureNowOnTV
text: Interesting info Luke. There is an audio sync issue on your to camera scenes and the audio is clipping (distorting) when in game etc.

author: @samhughes1747
text: It’s clearly graphitizing. You should also try tightening it up, if there are screw/posts. The comments on thermal cycling are valid too, but if you pull off the heatsink it’ll be way more gray than before.

author: @stevenfaber3896
text: Possible that your heating and cooling cycling of the thermal paste has allowed it to achieve more "squish" forgive me the term escapes me, so that there is even less air contact between the mating surfaces. Ambient does affect your results, but the consistency shows. Great work. See ya in another 6!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Possibly for sure! I have no reason to take it out just yet, I think an extra year would be good lol! Thanks mate. :)

author: @uksuperrascal
text: re rocket stove - Have you tried 3 meters of copper wire and 3 meters of iron wire joined as a pair at one end, insulated from the stove and each other, wound round the stove chimney, then when the stove is hot, measure the dc volts across the wires. I had heard this works ?  something to to do with thermal electron flow.

author: @totherarf
text:
You could get an estimation of Ambient temp effect by comparing your start temps (now and last year). It won't be exact because you have a heat source .... but it could give an indication!

A Year ago?  .... A whole year? ..... What devilry is this? Have I been eating lotus'?

Eather way, Nice one Luke!

author: @coldfinger459sub0
text:
Unfortunately ambient temperatures do make a large difference because the differences are not linear. What of energy to temperature raise ratio. 

       With the ambient temperature to the fin area of the heat sink you had a temperature that has the temperature rises the peak temperature will rise quicker compared to colder days and once you hit that threshold if you did it gets into the runaway region where you would never hit that on a cold or ambient day. . 

  This is why this type of test must be carried out in a temperature and humidity controlled room.
